title: ranunculus broteri
intro: Ranunculus broteri, also known as the Madeiran cobra Lily, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and saponins. The health benefits of Ranunculus broteri include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity.
